content:
Identified 2026-07-16, not yet run. All Phase-10 gap diagnosis so far (docs/phase10-gap-audit.md, ISS-001..010) has been plugin-vs-real-pedal-capture only. There is a second, independent, ALREADY-EXISTING reference that has never been used for this round of gap-hunting: docs/reference-fr-targets.md, the quantitative transcription of the author's SPICE sim graphs (per circuit.md: "the first-pass validation reference for every linear stage — available before any real capture").

Why this matters right now, specifically:
1. It is per-control and single-variable (e.g. "BASS knob alone, tone stack in isolation") where the NAM captures are all multi-knob (see the companion methodology note on cascade_analysis.py's confound) — it can isolate exactly what the captures can't.
2. It directly names the gate that ISS-009 (V1L C10/R14, netlists.md L5d) is conditioned on: "if the derived V1L wet-path LF misses FR §1, re-crop this node first." That gate has fired against captures; checking it against the SPICE targets too, independently, would either corroborate the C10 diagnosis or reveal the capture-side read was itself off.
3. It costs nothing new — no capture, no render-vs-capture alignment, just comparing the existing WDF/plugin FR (or the OfflineRender output) against numbers already transcribed in the repo.
4. Splits "is this a MODEL bug (disagrees with both SPICE and captures) vs a CALIBRATION bug (matches SPICE, disagrees with captures — i.e. a kInputRef/kOutputMakeup/kDryGain problem)" — the two families of gap need very different fixes, and right now every open issue (ISS-001..006, 008, 009) is unclassified on this axis.

Suggested first target: ISS-009 (V1L C10/R14 wet-path LF) — it already names the exact SPICE section (§1) to check, and a shape-only comparison (same treatment as gap_audit.py: grade Δ vs the SPICE curve, don't chase absolute level) needs no new tooling beyond what exists.

Second target once the method is proven: ISS-008 (V2 dry-path HF) candidate (a) — netlists.md V1's "IN_B -> direct (no cap) -> BLEND VR50.a" read implies no dry-path HF rolloff exists in the real circuit at all, which would mean the SPICE targets ALSO show no rolloff and this note's method can't resolve ISS-008 alone — worth checking anyway to rule it out cheaply before spending time on candidates (b)/(c).
title: Untested diagnostic: cross-check plugin FR against docs/reference-fr-targets.md (SPICE sim, per-control, isolates what captures can't)
